mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistically significant negative correlation with rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive forward returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold being breached.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with stable consumer price inflation indicates robust consumer demand without triggering monetary tightening fears, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal yields positive returns in < 50% of cases when retail sales rise and CPI growth remains below 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals improving supply-side capacity utilization and corporate output growth, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days specifically during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during post-shock recovery periods, or forward returns are negative in > 60% of acceleration instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC remains below 0.02, or payroll growth fails to predict positive forward returns in > 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 5% indicates a sudden contraction in construction demand and consumer confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 60% of instances following a >5% housing starts decline.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a rising federal funds rate signals an active monetary tightening cycle that increases borrowing costs and compresses equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint inflation-rate signal produces positive forward returns in > 55% of cases during tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product confirms broad-based economic expansion, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days during mid-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: GDP growth fails to predict positive forward returns in > 60% of quarterly observations during mid-cycle expansions.
